concerns: Row-level validation in parseCardCsv.ts already rejects unknown tiers and categories per-row. The plan's dataset-level checks for these in validateCardDeck will re-check the same constraints. This is fine as defense-in-depth but the plan should note that these checks serve as a safety net for cards constructed outside parseCardCsv (e.g. in tests), not as the primary validation path.

pairsWith validation (checking referenced card numbers exist in the dataset) is a reasonable addition but is not explicitly listed in the acceptance criteria. The plan correctly flags this as a risk — implement it but keep it separable so it can be dropped if scope is challenged.

The plan creates two separate files (validateCardDeck.ts and normalizeCardDeck.ts) for what could be a single module. This is acceptable given the project's preference for small pure functions, but consider whether a single file with two exported functions would be simpler to maintain.
